subject: revert OOM-420 to avoid helm 2.3+ upgrade
commit_message:
revert OOM-420 to avoid helm 2.3+ upgrade

Issue-ID: OOM-441

Change-Id: I657b9c169dcfc9a8320277f14b5b7908dd2cdf2a
Signed-off-by: Michael O'Brien <frank.obrien@amdocs.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Testing a revert so we can bring up onap-log on helm 2.3 like before

Patch Set 1:

Also testing vnc-portal which stopped working after a helm 2.3 upgrade

Patch Set 1:

vnc-portal and onap-log come up as long as we run rancher 1.6.10 (which runs helm 2.3.0) - see test results in jira

Patch Set 1:

onap-portal           vnc-portal-3680188324-2klp8             1/1       Running   0          51m

Patch Set 1:

onap-log              elasticsearch-1942187295-tbt04           1/1       Running            0          7m

Patch Set 1:

onap-portal           vnc-portal-3680188324-c3g25              0/1       Init:2/5           0          9m

Patch Set 1:

onap-portal           vnc-portal-3680188324-c3g25              1/1       Running            0          12m

Patch Set 1:

CD test results without patch (forced Rancher 1.6.10) - same as with patch on 1.6.11 for onap-log - but 1.6.11 still breaks vnc-portal

Patch Set 1:

Correction CD test results without patch don't fix log - just portal running 1.6.10

Patch Set 1:

http://jenkins.onap.info/job/oom-cd/177/console

Patch Set 1:

> vnc-portal and onap-log come up as long as we run rancher 1.6.10
 > (which runs helm 2.3.0) - see test results in jira

Why not install helm v2.7.2?
root@oom-demo-rancher:~# tar -zxvf helm-v2.7.2-linux-amd64.tar.gz
linux-amd64/
linux-amd64/README.md
linux-amd64/LICENSE
linux-amd64/helm
root@oom-demo-rancher:~# mv linux-amd64/helm /usr/local/bin/helm
root@oom-demo-rancher:~# helm version
Client: &version.Version{SemVer:"v2.7.2", GitCommit:"8478fb4fc723885b155c924d1c8c410b7a9444e6", GitTreeState:"clean"}
Server: &version.Version{SemVer:"v2.3.0", GitCommit:"d83c245fc324117885ed83afc90ac74afed271b4", GitTreeState:"clean"}
root@oom-demo-rancher:~# helm init --upgrade
$HELM_HOME has been configured at /root/.helm.

Tiller (the Helm server-side component) has been upgraded to the current version.
Happy Helming!

root@oom-demo-rancher:~# helm version
Client: &version.Version{SemVer:"v2.7.2", GitCommit:"8478fb4fc723885b155c924d1c8c410b7a9444e6", GitTreeState:"clean"}
Server: &version.Version{SemVer:"v2.7.2", GitCommit:"8478fb4fc723885b155c924d1c8c410b7a9444e6", GitTreeState:"clean"}

According to evidence attached in referenced issues, I see no reason to commit this change.

Patch Set 1:

Testing helm 2.7.1 server/client - hold of on merge

Patch Set 1:

Still failing with a helm 2.7.2 server/client upgrade - may be 1.8.3 vs running 1.7.7 issue

Patch Set 1:

we still need to push this so OPNFV and VMware can move on while we figure out the dependency for OOM-420

Patch Set 1:

> we still need to push this so OPNFV and VMware can move on while we
 > figure out the dependency for OOM-420

This change will prevent logs working in namespaces other than onap.

Patch Set 1:

yes, unavoidable for now - we go back to hardcoded onap namespace - however an upgrade to Helm requires a bit more testing and documentation first - when we have an upgrade procedure documented and tested then we can put the change in OOM-420 back - for now we need to unblock other OOM users

Patch Set 1:

> yes, unavoidable for now - we go back to hardcoded onap namespace -
 > however an upgrade to Helm requires a bit more testing and
 > documentation first - when we have an upgrade procedure documented
 > and tested then we can put the change in OOM-420 back - for now we
 > need to unblock other OOM users

I tested on openlab and it works well. 
I wonder what needs unblocking. 
As we move forward towards configuration management, we need the templating abilities in helm 2.6 and higher. Please do the effort to make rancher and helm 2.7 work for you. It should be straightforward.

Patch Set 1:

Borislav, I posted R1.6.11/helm 2.7.2 server/client testing 10 hours ago to the JIRA

Patch Set 1:

I don't appreciate the fact I need to lead the effort to verify your changes - there are at least 3 of us with the same results here - obviously there is something in your configuration that is different - we just need to track it down

Patch Set 1:

My env is AWS EC2, and VMWare VM on a MAC - I don't want to upgrade some of my other working 1.6.10/h2.3 envs right now as I need them for my own work - not verifying fixes

Patch Set 1:

Test setup = clean VM, install Rancher 1.6.11 (latest) - containing Helm 2.6.1

Patch Set 1:

Install helm 2.7.2 client

Patch Set 1:

upgrade helm - pushes server to helm 2.7.2 - note my kubectl is 1.8.3 not 1.7.7 like yours - this may be the issue

Patch Set 1:

3 of us get onap-log:es ram issue, vnc-portal init failure - that we don't see on 1.6.10/h2.3

Patch Set 1:

Fix for OOM-431 increases virtual memory - fixes only onap-log:elasticsearch

Patch Set 1:

we still need to fix vnc-portal - the other 3 failures are expected and OK
Env: 90G rackspace vm: Helm 2.7.2 (client+server), Rancher 1.6.11, kubectl 1.8.3

4 pending > 3 at the 52th 15 sec interval
onap-aaf              aaf-849d477595-r96lw                     0/1       Running            0          15m
onap-clamp            clamp-5489dd969f-7frjw                   0/1       CrashLoopBackOff   6          15m
onap-portal           vnc-portal-5b45665475-m9nd7              0/1       CrashLoopBackOff   7          17m
onap-vfc              vfc-workflow-ff78d878f-f4zjs             0/1       CrashLoopBackOff   7          15m

Patch Set 1:

Sorry guys, I was out the last couple of days. What's the decision here? Should we move forward with this. I agree with the fact we shouldn't enforce user to upgrade to a newer version of helm, if not well advertised through mailing list, at least.
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1:

to be clear - I think we definitely should move to helm 2.7 - just that we need to figure out the remaining issue with vnc-portal first

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Acutally, I think it's ok to upgrade in master, once properly advertised through mailing list!
That being said, we should not ask the end user to upgrade the helm version within service releases, this is very bad practice around our project.
Please review and merge the reverted patch in release-1.1.0 https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/24713/
Thanks
